channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Trump administration signs commercial nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia
item.description: The agreement gives American companies priority access to nuclear reactors and fuel to Saudi Arabia. It's expected to last decades and be worth billions of dollars.
item.content:encoded:
The agreement gives American companies priority access to nuclear reactors and fuel to Saudi Arabia. It's expected to last decades and be worth billions of dollars.

(Image credit: Andrew Harnik)
item.dc:creator: Shannon Bond

item.title: Why the U.S. and Saudi Arabia signed a nuclear agreement
item.description: NPR has confirmed a deal that would allow the U.S. to transfer nuclear technology to Saudi Arabia and allow the country to begin enriching uranium for civilian use.
item.content:encoded: NPR has confirmed a deal that would allow the U.S. to transfer nuclear technology to Saudi Arabia and allow the country to begin enriching uranium for civilian use.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Jarenwattananon

item.title: What to know about Ukraine's military shakeup
item.description: After a week of nationwide protests over the direction of Ukraine's military strategy in the ongoing war with Russia, the Ukrainian military has a new commander, Mykhailo Drapatyi. Here's what to know.
item.content:encoded:
After a week of nationwide protests over the direction of Ukraine's military strategy in the ongoing war with Russia, the Ukrainian military has a new commander, Mykhailo Drapatyi. Here's what to know.

(Image credit: Paula Bronstein for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Joanna Kakissis

item.title: Greetings from Jerusalem, whose holy sites host some of the oldest colonies of nesting swifts
item.description: The scythe-winged birds have nested in the cracks of the Western Wall and other holy sites in Jerusalem for thousands of years.
item.content:encoded: The scythe-winged birds have nested in the cracks of the Western Wall and other holy sites in Jerusalem for thousands of years.
item.dc:creator: Ruth Sherlock

item.title: Trump to attend dignified transfer of fallen soldiers. And, Hegseth testifies on Iran
item.description: Trump will attend the dignified transfer of U.S. service members killed in the Middle East. And, Pete Hegseth is requesting billions from Congress to help with the rising cost of the war in Iran.
item.content:encoded:
Trump will attend the dignified transfer of U.S. service members killed in the Middle East. And, Pete Hegseth is requesting billions from Congress to help with the rising cost of the war in Iran.

(Image credit: Saul Loeb)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: U.S. and Iranian attacks continue as tensions mount on a key waterway in the Red Sea
item.description: The fighting comes as the Trump administration is seeking billions of dollars more from Congress for the war with Iran, which Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth says has already cost $37.5 billion.
item.content:encoded:
The fighting comes as the Trump administration is seeking billions of dollars more from Congress for the war with Iran, which Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth says has already cost $37.5 billion.

(Image credit: Atta Kenare)
item.dc:creator: Jane Arraf

item.title: 'It's all literally rubble': Lebanese poet returns to see what has become of her home
item.description: A village destroyed. A home erased. One poet returns to remember what was lost.
item.content:encoded: A village destroyed. A home erased. One poet returns to remember what was lost.
item.dc:creator: Ruth Sherlock

item.title: Group of Indigenous Taiwanese row over 100 miles to recreate their ancestors' journey
item.description: Indigenous people in Taiwan complete a journey to the Philippines in a traditional rowboat, recreating a journey their ancestors made thousands of years ago.
item.content:encoded: Indigenous people in Taiwan complete a journey to the Philippines in a traditional rowboat, recreating a journey their ancestors made thousands of years ago.
item.dc:creator: Jan Camenzind Broomby

item.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy fires top military commander amid ongoing protests
item.description: Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskyy fires the country's army chief, just days after he dismissed the defense minister. Those two key figures had been feuding with each other.
item.content:encoded: Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskyy fires the country's army chief, just days after he dismissed the defense minister. Those two key figures had been feuding with each other.
item.dc:creator: Greg Myre

item.title: French lawmakers approve a sweeping social media ban for children under 15
item.description: The move makes France the first country in the European Union to pass a blanket ban on platforms as concerns grow worldwide over the harmful effects of digital content on kids.
item.content:encoded:
The move makes France the first country in the European Union to pass a blanket ban on platforms as concerns grow worldwide over the harmful effects of digital content on kids.

(Image credit: Nicolas Garriga)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
